# Interval Estimation

Intervals only make sense together with the procedure that constructs them and with
the notion of coverage. This chapter should therefore explain what guarantee stands
behind a given interval rather than collect formulas mechanically.

Later this section will host Neyman constructions, profile-likelihood intervals, and
boundary regimes where naive symmetric formulas stop behaving well.

::: {.notebook-card}
### Companion notebook

The companion notebook contains the simplest interval example for a proportion and
leaves a natural place for future coverage studies.

<div class="notebook-actions">
  <a class="notebook-link notebook-link-primary" href="../notebooks/intervals_demo.qmd">Open notebook</a>
</div>
:::
